channel.title: AI Engineering on More's awesome website
channel.description: Recent content in AI Engineering on More's awesome website

item.title: Notes on the Foundation of Large Models
item.author: morthan@qq.com (Morethan)
item.description:
Notes written after reading the textbook “Foundation of Large Models” from Zhejiang University

Preface
#

The development of large model technology can be described as “rapidly evolving,” with seemingly groundbreaking advancements happening every day. 🤔

item.title: Practical LLM Training
item.author: morthan@qq.com (Morethan)
item.description:
Once you understand how a network architecture is implemented, a natural question arises: how do I actually train a model like this? This section compiles all sorts of engineering know-how that answers that very question.

Preface
#

Large model architectures come in all shapes and sizes, but if you really want to understand one, you have to get your hands dirty. There are plenty of high-quality online courses on YouTube to learn from— CS336: Language Modeling from Scratch being a prime example—so this article essentially serves as my notes on CS 336.

item.title: Notes on LLM Engineering Practices
item.author: morthan@qq.com (Morethan)
item.description:
This blog post is the zeroth article in the “AI Engineering” series. Besides serving as an index for the entire series, it also summarizes key knowledge points related to practical large model applications.

Introduction
#

Currently, large model technology is developing rapidly. Open-source base models like DeepSeek are gradually catching up with closed-source models, and general-purpose large models are achieving increasingly higher accuracy across various tasks, pushing their capability limits higher.
